T1.description: Primary official or near-primary sources. Highest authority for verification and cluster primacy.

T1_5.description: High-quality secondary official channels or strong market media. Useful for discovery, but noisier than T1.

T2.description: Discovery-oriented media, sector press, and KOL sources. Useful for recall, but not ideal as final primary evidence.

global_macro_geopolitics.description: Default broad scan for geopolitics, macro, policy, and cross-asset developments.

china_market_policy.description: China and Hong Kong policy, market structure, and financing environment.

technology_ai_semiconductor.description: Technology and AI coverage for investors, not consumer-tech chatter.

capital_markets_transactions.description: IPO, refinancing, M&A, credit, and transaction-related developments.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.notes: Default first-pass source for broad discovery and factual verification.

sources.name: Bloomberg
sources.notes: Strong for markets, financing, and cross-asset implications.

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.notes: Good for investor framing and policy context, but often not the first verification source.

sources.name: Hugging Face
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public blog RSS feed. The main site also exposes a broad sitemap, while blog articles are publicly accessible for full-text extraction.

sources.name: Latent Space
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public Substack posts API. Feed and sitemap are also available, and public posts expose full body_html suitable for extraction.

sources.name: Lenny's Newsletter
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public Substack posts API. Feed and sitemap are also available. Public announcement-style posts expose full body_html, but many core deep-dive posts are only_paid and surface preview metadata only.

sources.name: GitHub Trending
sources.notes: Trending is a ranking and discovery surface rather than a long-form article source. Primary intake uses the public daily trending HTML page.

sources.name: GitHub Issues Trends
sources.notes: A trend and query surface rather than a traditional article source. Primary intake uses the public GitHub search/issues API to rank recent issues by comment activity.

sources.name: Google Trends
sources.notes: A trend/discovery surface. Primary intake uses the public trending RSS feed and preserves rank_position, rank_section, and approx_traffic.

sources.name: Wall Street Journal
sources.notes: Useful for transaction and corporate strategy context.

sources.name: 华尔街见闻
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public information-flow API. The article/member detail API also exposes HTML content, making it suitable for both discovery and optional full-text enrichment.

sources.name: 同花顺财经热榜
sources.notes: A ranking/discovery surface. Primary intake uses the current homepage hot-rank HTML and preserves rank_position for downstream use.

sources.name: Wind资讯
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public Wind News AJAX list endpoint after establishing a site session. Detail content is available through a second public AJAX endpoint and can be used for full-text extraction.

sources.name: Associated Press
sources.notes: Helpful for breaking geopolitical and event coverage.

sources.name: Axios
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the official RSS feed. Full-text capability relies on RSS content because direct article pages are challenge-blocked in the current environment.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.notes: Useful for fast market-facing coverage and public clips, but not the default primary evidence source.

sources.name: Federal Reserve
sources.notes: Primary source for Fed policy, speeches, minutes, and balance sheet actions.

sources.name: European Central Bank
sources.notes: Primary source for eurozone monetary policy.

sources.name: U.S. Treasury
sources.notes: Important for sanctions, sovereign finance, and policy actions.

sources.name: PBOC
sources.notes: Primary source for China liquidity operations and monetary signals.

sources.name: National Bureau of Statistics of China
sources.notes: Primary source for China CPI, PPI, PMI-linked releases, industrial and retail data.

sources.name: CSRC
sources.notes: Primary source for listing policy, financing rules, and securities supervision.

sources.name: MOFCOM
sources.notes: Primary for trade policy, investment review, and commerce-related measures.

sources.name: NDRC
sources.notes: Important for industrial policy, capacity, energy, and project approvals.

sources.name: MIIT
sources.notes: Important for manufacturing, semiconductors, telecom, and industrial upgrades.

sources.name: SAMR
sources.notes: Important for antitrust, competition policy, recalls, and enforcement actions.

sources.name: CAC
sources.notes: Primary source for data governance and platform-related regulation.

sources.name: HKEX
sources.notes: Primary source for Hong Kong IPOs, listing filings, and market notices.

sources.name: Shanghai Stock Exchange
sources.notes: Primary source for A-share market notices and listing-related developments.

sources.name: Shenzhen Stock Exchange
sources.notes: Primary source for A-share market notices and listing-related developments.

sources.name: SEC Filings
sources.notes: Primary source for U.S. IPOs, secondary offerings, M&A filings, and governance events.

sources.name: Company IR Pages
sources.notes: Primary source for deal announcements, strategic updates, and official corporate disclosures.

sources.name: Caixin
sources.notes: High-value China financial journalism; strong for policy, finance, and industry readthrough.

sources.name: Yicai
sources.notes: Useful for China economic and business discovery.

sources.name: Cailian Press
sources.notes: Fast-moving China market and sector coverage. Good recall, weaker as primary evidence.

sources.name: Securities Times
sources.notes: Useful for A-share financing, policy signaling, and industry themes.

sources.name: OpenAI Blog
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the official OpenAI News RSS feed. Filter to canonical `/index/` article URLs because the feed mixes in Academy and other non-news surfaces. Sitemaps are useful for catalog validation, but not as the real-time primary inlet because many entries get fresh lastmod timestamps unrelated to first publication. Normalize feed links to the trailing-slash canonical article URL for stable full-text fetches.

sources.name: 36氪
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the official RSS feed. Public article pages expose visible articleDetail content for full-text extraction. No clear member-only article structure was identified in the tested public feed sample.

sources.name: a16z Blog
sources.notes: Discovery uses the public news-content listing. Article pages expose published time, summary metadata, and public full text, though footer legal disclaimers require source-specific cleanup.

sources.name: Anthropic News
sources.notes: Primary source for Anthropic releases and policy statements. Discovery uses the public /news listing; article pages are publicly accessible for full-text extraction.

sources.name: Google Blog
sources.notes: Useful for major Google AI and infrastructure updates.

sources.name: BBC
sources.notes: Broad global coverage with strong breaking-news utility. Best used for discovery and context, then cross-checked with higher-priority primary or financial sources when needed.

sources.name: TechCrunch
sources.notes: Primary intake can use the public WordPress JSON API; RSS and sitemap remain useful as fallback discovery layers.

sources.name: Y Combinator
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the official YC Blog RSS feed, which includes title, canonical article URL, timestamps, categories, and content:encoded body HTML. The site-wide sitemap is broad and noisy, so it is useful only as a light catalog check rather than a real-time discovery inlet.

sources.name: Semafor
sources.notes: Primary intake currently uses the official RSS feed; sitemap is a useful backup discovery layer for recent article URLs.

sources.name: Semianalysis
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the newsletter.semianalysis.com Substack archive API, which surfaces current posts, timestamps, canonical URLs, and body_html. The legacy semianalysis.com WordPress JSON exists but appears stale relative to the active newsletter flow.

sources.name: Stratechery
sources.notes: Primary intake can use the public WordPress JSON API; RSS and sitemap are also available. Public metadata is stable, but article access mixes free essays, newsletters, and member-only teasers, so full-text coverage is mixed.

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the official RSS feed, with article-page __NEXT_DATA__ used to enrich dates, summaries, and optional body extraction.

sources.name: South China Morning Post
sources.notes: Primary intake currently uses official RSS feeds; article pages remain publicly accessible for optional full-text extraction.

sources.name: Politico
sources.notes: Primary intake uses official RSS feeds. Full-text capability relies on content:encoded embedded in the feed because direct article pages are often challenge-blocked.

sources.name: The Information
sources.notes: High-signal tech reporting, but public home/feed/sitemap/search/graphql entrances currently return Cloudflare 403 in this environment. Google News can surface recent headlines, but stable original-link recovery is not yet available.

sources.name: Expert KOLs
sources.notes: Disabled by default. Only use as discovery support, never as final primary evidence.

sources.name: 赛博禅心
sources.notes: User-preferred WeChat discovery source. Use for idea discovery only; verify with stronger sources before selection.

sources.name: 数字生命卡兹克
sources.notes: User-preferred WeChat discovery source. Use for discovery only.

sources.name: 泽平宏观
sources.notes: User-preferred macro discovery source. Useful for topic framing, but verify claims with primary or stronger media sources.

sources.name: ZeroHedge
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public full RSS feed. Public article pages are accessible and generally support full-text extraction.

sources.name: Unusual Whales
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the /news page __NEXT_DATA__ state. Article pages expose full body HTML in embedded state and support full-text extraction.

sources.name: TradingView News
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public TradingView news page and article-page embedded story payload. The generic /feed endpoint is primarily for ideas, not the curated news stream.

sources.name: Discord Blog
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the official Discord Blog RSS feed. Public article pages are accessible and support full-text extraction.

sources.name: Telegram Blog
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public Telegram Blog listing page. Public article pages expose published timestamps and support full-text extraction.

sources.name: LessWrong
sources.notes: Official feed contains rich post descriptions that can serve as lightweight fulltext input.

sources.name: Lobsters
sources.notes: Official RSS is the best lightweight discovery layer. The source itself is a discussion forum, so canonical URLs point to Lobsters discussion pages while source_url preserves the original linked article.

sources.name: 格隆汇
sources.notes: Primary intake uses the public /news listing page and article-page embedded __NUXT__ state for timestamps, summary, and body content.
